title: Using ggerror
text: ggplot2 ships several error geoms. ggerror started wrapper simplify usage replacing x/y-min/max inputs one aesthetic: error. doesn’t just simplify API, also shifts focus actual intention: visualize magnitude error extends specified value (central metric; vignette("use-cases")). ggerror longer just wrapper; offers extra functionality compared ggplot2’s core error geoms. vignette demonstrates basic usage package. stat_error() summaries sign_aware errors, see vignette("use-cases").

title: Using ggerror
text: use airquality — Daily air quality measurements New York, May September 1973. dataset 153 rows 6 columns: four continuous measurements, plus Month Day.
code: data("airquality"); airq <- airquality  # It wouldn't be an R workflow without minimal data cleaning... day_in_month <- function(day_in_month, month, year) {   days_abbr <- format(as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-%02d", year, month, day_in_month)), "%a")   factor(days_abbr, levels = c("Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"), ordered = TRUE)  } airq$Day <- day_in_month(airq$Day, airq$Month, 1973) airq$Month <- factor(airq$Month, labels = month.abb[5:9])   # summary table, grouped by month with Temp's means and standard deviation  aq_monthly_avg <- data.frame(   Month = unique(airq$Month),   Temp = tapply(airq$Temp, airq$Month, mean),   sd_temp_monthly   = tapply(airq$Temp, airq$Month, sd) )  p <- ggplot(aq_monthly_avg, aes(Temp, Month))

title: Using ggerror
text: single error aesthetic enough — ggerror infers orientation data (: numeric x axis, discrete y axis) picks geom_errorbar default base geom.  data, pinned geom_error_pointrange() wrapper:  data, swapped via error_geom argument:  💡 Tip: error_geom just argument, can iterate functionally:
code: p +    geom_error(aes(error = sd_temp_monthly),              width = 0.4) +   labs(title = "p + geom_error(aes(error = sd_temp_monthly))",        caption = "p <- ggplot(aq_monthly_avg, aes(Temp, Month))"        ) p +   geom_error_pointrange(aes(error = sd_temp_monthly),                         size = 0.8,                         shape = "x") +   labs(title = "p + geom_error_pointrange(aes(error = sd_temp_monthly))") p +   geom_error(aes(error = sd_temp_monthly),              error_geom = "linerange") +   labs(title = "geom_error(error_geom = \"linerange\")") purrr::map(c("errorbar", "linerange", "crossbar", "pointrange"),            ~ p + geom_error(aes(error = sd_temp_monthly), error_geom = .x))

title: Using ggerror
text: error_neg error_pos extend opposite directions regardless orientation. ’re useful dispersion measure side carries different meaning.  Deciding counts negative positive error discretion. Per-side styling extends color, fill, linewidth, linetype, alpha, width. Pass either side-specific form (color_neg, width_pos) shared form (color, width).
code: may_week <- subset(airq[1:7,], Month == 'May')  may_summary <- data.frame(   Day = may_week$Day,   Temp = may_week$Temp,   dist2min = may_week$Temp - min(may_week$Temp, na.rm = TRUE),   dist2max = max(may_week$Temp, na.rm = TRUE) - may_week$Temp ) # Each Temp point now has its distance from the minimum Temp in the   # dataset, and its distance from the maximum Temp in the dataset. ggplot(may_summary, aes(x = Temp, y = Day)) +   geom_error(aes(error_neg =  dist2min,                  error_pos = dist2max),              error_geom = "pointrange",              color_neg = "steelblue",              color_pos = "firebrick",              linewidth  = 1              ) +   labs(title = "geom_error(error_neg = dist2min, error_pos = dist2max)") ggplot(may_summary, aes(Temp, Day)) +   geom_error(aes(error_neg = dist2min,                  error_pos = dist2max),              error_geom = "crossbar",              fill_neg = "#d97757", fill_pos = "#ffaa66",              width_neg = 0.3,     width_pos = 0.6) +   labs(title = "crossbar with per-side fills + widths")

title: Using ggerror
text: Sometimes care mostly errors towards one side, dismiss uninteresting. example, observation falls threshold. Set unused side NA ggerror suppresses cap stem side automatically.  Remember: errors simply magnitude. don’t even treated errors, like last couple examples, treated distances arbitrary value (one-sided example: distance temperature minimum temperature measured). Passing 0 instead NA works, discouraged, several reasons: 1. NA explicit 0. 2. Since 0 treated value, won’t automatically cap undesired side error bars, example. See vignette("use-cases") advanced options.
code: ggplot(may_summary, aes(Temp, Day)) +   geom_error(aes(error_neg = dist2min, error_pos = NA),              color = "steelblue",              linewidth = 1,              linetype = 9) +   geom_point(size = 1.5) +   labs(title = "geom_error(aes(error_neg=dist2min, error_pos = NA))")

title: Using ggerror
text: know , need, new error geom, please open issue GitHub repository. first motivation simplify heck error geoms, reducing aesthetics single error aesthetic. rest (asymmetric, one-sided, etc) just niceties added along way.

title: Use cases: stats and residuals
text: use airquality — Daily air quality measurements New York, May September 1973. dataset 153 rows 6 columns: 4 continuous measurements, Month Day. airquality contains daily New York air-quality measurements May September 1973. main continuous variables Ozone, Solar.R, Wind, Temp; Month Day identify measurement taken. Let’s use show two use cases: plotting sign-aware residual plot summarizing raw data stat_error().
code: data("airquality"); airq <- airquality  # It wouldn't be an R workflow without minimal data cleaning... day_in_month <- function(day_in_month, month, year) {   days_abbr <- format(as.Date(sprintf("%d-%02d-%02d", year, month, day_in_month)), "%a")   factor(days_abbr, levels = c("Mon","Tue","Wed","Thu","Fri","Sat","Sun"), ordered = TRUE) } airq$Day <- day_in_month(airq$Day, airq$Month, 1973) airq$Month <- factor(airq$Month, labels = month.abb[5:9])

title: Use cases: stats and residuals
text: sign_aware = TRUE interprets sign error direction: positive values extend positive axis direction, negative negative.

what: Let’s fit a linear model and see how Temp can be explained by Wind.
title: Use cases: stats and residuals
text: Let us complement usual ggplot2 workflow plotting residuals versus true values:  Flexible styling: ggerror provides us per-direction styling (color) well global styles (alpha, linewidth, etc.). interpret plot . may want color direction differently, show direction bar extends, rather sign error. Experiment tweaking either actual color value, neg/pos suffix. orientation = "x" axes numeric, ggerror can’t infer direction bar extend, pass orientation = "x" (extends bar along y axis) explicitly. one axis discrete, orientation inferred. NA values vs 0: NA value draws nothing, triggers row-indexed warning. ggplot2’s ecosystem, usually exclude invalid (missing infinite) values plotting. 0 treated value, “draws” bar length zero (basically, nothing). Using NA comes handy want explicitly draw one-sided bars (see vignette("ggerror")).
code: model   <- lm(Temp ~ Wind, data = airq) airq_fit <- cbind(airquality[,c('Temp','Wind')],                   predicted = predict(model),                   residuals = resid(model)                  ) ggplot(airq_fit, aes(x = Wind, y = predicted)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.4, colour = "grey40") +   geom_point(aes(y = Temp), alpha = 0.5) +   geom_error(aes(error = residuals),              sign_aware  = TRUE,              orientation = "x",              color_pos  = "firebrick",              color_neg  = "steelblue",              linewidth   = 0.5,              alpha       = 0.85) +   labs(title = paste(     "[1] residuals = resid(lm(Temp ~ Wind))",     "[2] geom_error(error = residuals, sign_aware = TRUE)",     sep = "\n"   ))

title: Use cases: stats and residuals
text: stat_error() follows ggplot2’s fun.data contract: pass summary function computes y, ymin, ymax per group. Regardless orientation plot, summary function must return data frame exact column names. default summary function mean_se (mean ± one standard error): ggerror ships two summary functions. just saw default, mean_se. second mean_ci computes mean 95% confidence interval error bars.  conf.int argument controls CI width — 0.95 default, valid probability (0, 1) works:  Note: combine multiple summary geoms. ggerror compatible ggplot2 (core) ecosystem.
code: ggplot(airq, aes(Month, Temp)) +   stat_error(width = 0.2) +   stat_summary(geom='point') +   labs(title = "stat_error() with default = mean_se") ggplot(airq, aes(Month, Temp)) +   stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", error_geom = "pointrange") +   labs(title = 'stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", error_geom = "pointrange")') ggplot(airq, aes(Month, Temp)) +   stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", conf.int = 0.99,              error_geom = "linerange") +    stat_summary(geom="point") +   labs(title = 'stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", conf.int = 0.99)')

what: Custom summary function: median ± mean absolute deviation
title: Use cases: stats and residuals
text: shall limited imagination. Create custom summary functions, long obey rules mentioned earlier (returned object must data frame three columns: y, ymin, ymax one numeric row). R’s mad() computes median absolute deviation median. Let us use mean absolute deviation (median), call mae (e error, avoid naming conflicts). median center, mean absolute deviation median spread: md(x)=median(x),mae(x)=1n∑=1n|xi−md(x)| \mathrm{md}(x) = \mathrm{median}(x), \qquad \mathrm{mae}(x) = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{=1}^{n}\left|x_i - \mathrm{md}(x)\right| y=md(x),ymin=md(x)−mae(x),ymax=md(x)+mae(x) y = \mathrm{md}(x), \qquad y_{\min} = \mathrm{md}(x) - \mathrm{mae}(x), \qquad y_{\max} = \mathrm{md}(x) + \mathrm{mae}(x)  Custom functions can take extra parameters — stat_error() forwards matching named arguments way used using ... argument. example, multiply error 2, ’d write stat_error(fun = mae_summary, scale_by = 2).  Aside — geom_error(stat = "error", fun = ...) layer-level equivalent stat_error(), default uses stat = "identity".
code: mae_summary <- function(my_vec, scale_by = 1) {   md  <- median(my_vec)   mae <- mean(abs(my_vec - md)) * scale_by   data.frame(y = md, ymin = md - mae, ymax = md + mae) }  ggplot(airq, aes(Month, Temp)) +   stat_error(fun = mae_summary, error_geom = "crossbar",              fill = "grey90") +   labs(title = "stat_error(fun = median ± mean(|x − median|))") ggplot(airq, aes(Month, Temp)) +   stat_error(fun = mae_summary, scale_by = 2, error_geom = "crossbar") +   stat_summary(geom = "point")

code: @Manual{,   title = {ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2'},   author = {Yann Cohen},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/iamYannC/ggerror}, }

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
text: ggerror simplifies ggplot2’s error geoms introduces asymetric error bars customization. Instead wiring ymin / ymax xmin / xmax hand, supply error (error_neg + error_pos) ggerror rest . can simple providing single error argument, yet offer full customization options per-side styling.

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
code: install.packages("ggerror") # 0.3.0  pak::pak("iamyannc/ggerror") # 1.0.0 - better error messages & stat_error & sign_aware!  library(ggplot2) library(ggerror)  p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point()  # Symmetric error bars, using default geom errorbar p + geom_error(aes(error = drat))  # Asymmetric error bars, using geom_error_pointrange and per-side styling p + geom_error_pointrange(aes(error_neg = drat / 2, error_pos = drat, linetype_neg = "dashed"))  # One-sided error bars, using the error_geom argument p + geom_error(error_geom = "linerange", aes(error_neg = NA, error_pos = drat))

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
text: detailed examples symmetric, asymmetric, one-sided, per-side styling, see vignette("ggerror").

title: Extended Error Geoms for ggplot2
text: package developed assistance AI tools. code reviewed author, remains responsible quality. Ideas new geoms welcome.

what: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: thin wrapper around ggplot2::geom_errorbar(), ggplot2::geom_linerange(), ggplot2::geom_crossbar(), ggplot2::geom_pointrange() accepts single error aesthetic figures orientation data. asymmetric errors, use error_neg + error_pos instead error.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
code: geom_error(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   stat = "identity",   position = "identity",   ...,   error_geom = "errorbar",   orientation = NA,   sign_aware = FALSE,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )  geom_error_linerange(..., error_geom)  geom_error_crossbar(..., error_geom)  geom_error_pointrange(..., error_geom)

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created ggplot2::aes(). data data displayed layer. stat statistical transformation use data. Defaults "identity". position Position adjustment. ... arguments passed ggplot2::layer(). error_geom One "errorbar" (default), "linerange", "crossbar", "pointrange". Chooses ggplot2 error geom geom_error() dispatches hood. orientation Either NA (default; inferred data), "x" (vertical error), "y" (horizontal error). sign_aware TRUE, signed values error routed per row: positive values extend bar positive direction, negative values extend negative direction, opposite side suppressed. Useful residual plots x/y fitted value bar extends toward observed value. Incompatible stat = "error". Default FALSE. na.rm FALSE, missing values removed warning. show.legend Logical. layer included legends? inherit.aes FALSE, overrides default aesthetics.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: Session-level knobs 0 -> NA migration. Set via options(): ggerror.silent_zero_warning — TRUE suppresses soft deprecation fired error_neg error_pos set 0 (encouraged set NA). Default FALSE. ggerror.zero_threshold — Numeric absolute tolerance zero-value detection. Values magnitude threshold treated exactly zero, triggering warning. Defaults 1e-8.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
text: geom_error() requires x, y, one : error — symmetric half-width applied along non-categorical axis. error_neg error_pos — asymmetric; bar extends error_neg negative direction error_pos positive direction along non-categorical axis. one-sided bar, set unused side NA — cap, stem, shared-bound cap side suppressed. Mixing error error_neg / error_pos error, providing one asymmetric pair. Fixed per-side styling can supplied ... _neg _pos suffixes colour, fill, linewidth, linetype, alpha, width. fixed scalar parameters, mapped aesthetics.

title: Error bars with automatic orientation — geom_error
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error = drat))   ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error = drat), error_geom = "pointrange")   # Asymmetric: bar extends drat/2 below and drat above each point ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error_neg = drat / 2, error_pos = drat))   # One-sided: set the unused side to NA (cap + stem auto-suppressed) ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error_neg = NA, error_pos = drat))   # Summarise raw data: mean +/- SE per group (see also stat_error()) ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   geom_error(stat = "error", error_geom = "pointrange")   # Signed residual plot: bar extends from fitted toward observed model <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars) ggplot(mtcars, aes(fitted(model), mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(aes(error = resid(model)),              sign_aware = TRUE, orientation = "x")   # Style the negative and positive halves separately ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, rownames(mtcars))) +   geom_point() +   geom_error(     aes(error_neg = drat / 2, error_pos = drat),     colour_neg = "steelblue",     colour_pos = "firebrick"   )

what: ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2' — ggerror-package
title: ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2' — ggerror-package
text: Extends 'ggplot2' error geoms. geom_error() accepts 'error' aesthetic auto-inference orientation. also supports 'error_neg' 'error_pos' asymmetric cases, full control aesthetics per side, color, width etc... package also includes vignette covering main usecases - symmetric, asymmetric, one-sided, per-side styling.

title: ggerror: Extended Error Geoms for 'ggplot2' — ggerror-package
text: Maintainer: Yann Cohen yannco5@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
text: stat_error() computes error bounds raw observation-level data using ggplot2's fun.data contract. geom_error() expects pre- computed error columns, stat_error() summarises y (x, orientation horizontal) within group via function supplied fun.

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
code: stat_error(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   geom = NULL,   position = "identity",   ...,   fun = "mean_se",   fun.args = list(),   error_geom = "errorbar",   orientation = NA,   na.rm = FALSE,   conf.int = 0.95,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
text: mapping, data, position, show.legend, inherit.aes Standard ggplot2 layer arguments. geom geom render summary . Defaults GeomErrorStat, reuses geom_error()'s draw path. ... Additional parameters. Names match fun's formals (name, fun accepts ...) forwarded fun; remainder go geom_error() per-side styling (colour_neg, width_pos, …) standard aesthetics. fun One "mean_se" (default, uses ggplot2::mean_se()), "mean_ci" (mean 95% normal-theory CI via stats::qt(); Hmisc dependency), function taking numeric vector returning single-row data.frame columns y, ymin, ymax. fun.args Named list extra arguments pass fun. Merged ... arguments whose names match fun's formals; fun.args wins collision. error_geom One "errorbar" (default), "linerange", "crossbar", "pointrange". orientation NA (default, inferred), "x", "y". na.rm TRUE, drop NA values summarised axis applying fun. conf.int Confidence level forwarded fun function accepts conf.int argument (e.g. fun = "mean_ci" custom fun.data formal). Ignored funs declare , safe leave default using fun = "mean_se".

title: Summarising stat for geom_error() — stat_error
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) + stat_error() #> `stat_error()` using fun = "mean_se".   ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", error_geom = "pointrange") #> `stat_error()` using fun = "mean_ci" and conf.int = 0.95.   # 90% CI with NA-tolerant summarising: ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) +   stat_error(fun = "mean_ci", conf.int = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE)

title: ggerror 1.0.0
text: New stat_error() summarises raw observation-level data error bounds. Accepts fun = "mean_se" (default), "mean_ci" (95% normal- theory CI, Hmisc dep), custom function following ggplot2’s fun.data contract. Also available via geom_error(stat = "error"). New sign_aware = TRUE routes signed per-row values (typically residuals) one-sided bars whose direction encodes sign. Enables one-layer lm() residual plots. aes(error_neg = NA, error_pos = ...) now canonical idiom one-sided bars — cap stem NA side auto-suppress. Passing 0 still renders emits soft deprecation warning; silence options(ggerror.silent_zero_warning = TRUE) tune detection threshold via options(ggerror.zero_threshold = ...). New diagnostics: NA values symmetric error now warn row indices (class ggerror_warn_error_na); negative values without sign_aware abort row indices migration suggestion (class ggerror_error_negative_error_aes). New vignette lm-residuals covering stat_error() sign_aware lm() residual-plot demo.

title: ggerror 0.4.0
text: Added pkgdown site https://iamyannc.github.io/ggerror/.

title: ggerror 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 ggerror now includes complete aestetic support one sided error bars. Added fixed per-side styling parameters error bars: colour_neg / colour_pos, fill_neg / fill_pos, linewidth_neg / linewidth_pos, linetype_neg / linetype_pos, alpha_neg / alpha_pos, width_neg / width_pos. Fixed bug 0.2.0: supplying one-sided error bars, unused side’s width can set 0, default, example geom_error(aes(error_neg = 0, error_pos = se), width_neg = 0). Added vignette("ggerror") covering symmetric, asymmetric, one-sided, per-side styling workflows.

title: ggerror 0.2.0
text: New error_neg / error_pos aesthetics asymmetric error bars. bar extends error_neg negative direction error_pos positive direction along non-categorical axis. One-sided bars supported setting unused side 0 explicitly (e.g. aes(error_neg = 0, error_pos = se) upward-bar). existing symmetric error aesthetic unchanged. Combining error either error_neg / error_pos, providing one asymmetric pair, raises error.

title: ggerror 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Provides geom_error() (plus geom_error_linerange(), geom_error_crossbar(), geom_error_pointrange()) wrapping ggplot2’s error geoms behind single error aesthetic automatic orientation inference.
